chapters.zh: 起初，神创造天地。
chapters.en: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.

chapters.zh: 地是空虚混沌，渊面黑暗；神的灵运行在水面上。
chapters.en: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

chapters.zh: 神说：要有光，就有了光。
chapters.en: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

chapters.zh: 神看光是好的，就把光暗分开了。
chapters.en: And God saw the light, that good: and God divided the light from the darkness.

chapters.zh: 神称光为昼，称暗为夜。有晚上，有早晨，这是头一日。
chapters.en: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.

chapters.zh: 神说：诸水之间要有空气，将水分为上下。
chapters.en: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.

chapters.zh: 神就造出空气，将空气以下的水、空气以上的水分开了。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which under the firmament from the waters which above the firmament: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 神称空气为天。有晚上，有早晨，是第二日。
chapters.en: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

chapters.zh: 神说：天下的水要聚在一处，使旱地露出来。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry appear: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 神称旱地为地，称水的聚处为海。神看著是好的。
chapters.en: And God called the dry Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that good.

chapters.zh: 神说：地要发生青草和结种子的菜蔬，并结果子的树木，各从其类，果子都包著核。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 于是地发生了青草和结种子的菜蔬，各从其类；并结果子的树木，各从其类；果子都包著核。神看著是好的。
chapters.en: And the earth brought forth grass, herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed in itself, after his kind: and God saw that good.

chapters.zh: 有晚上，有早晨，是第三日。
chapters.en: And the evening and the morning were the third day.

chapters.zh: 神说：天上要有光体，可以分昼夜，作记号，定节令、日子、年岁，
chapters.en: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:

chapters.zh: 并要发光在天空，普照在地上。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 于是神造了两个大光，大的管昼，小的管夜，又造众星，
chapters.en: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: the stars also.

chapters.zh: 就把这些光摆列在天空，普照在地上，
chapters.en: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,

chapters.zh: 管理昼夜，分别明暗。神看著是好的。
chapters.en: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that good.

chapters.zh: 有晚上，有早晨，是第四日。
chapters.en: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.

chapters.zh: 神说：水要多多滋生有生命的物；要有雀鸟飞在地面以上，天空之中。
chapters.en: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.

chapters.zh: 神就造出大鱼和水中所滋生各样有生命的动物，各从其类；又造出各样飞鸟，各从其类。神看著是好的。
chapters.en: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that good.

chapters.zh: 神就赐福给这一切，说：滋生繁多，充满海中的水；雀鸟也要多生在地上。
chapters.en: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.

chapters.zh: 有晚上，有早晨，是第五日。
chapters.en: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.

chapters.zh: 神说：地要生出活物来，各从其类；牲畜、昆虫、地上的野兽，各从其类。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 于是神造出野兽，各从其类；牲畜，各从其类；地上一切昆虫，各从其类。神看著是好的。
chapters.en: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that good.

chapters.zh: 神说：我们要照著我们的形像、按著我们的样式造人，使他们管理海里的鱼、空中的鸟、地上的牲畜，和全地，并地上所爬的一切昆虫。
chapters.en: And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 神就照著自己的形像造人，乃是照著他的形像造男造女。
chapters.en: So God created man in his image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

chapters.zh: 神就赐福给他们，又对他们说：要生养众多，遍满地面，治理这地，也要管理海里的鱼、空中的鸟，和地上各样行动的活物。
chapters.en: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 神说：看哪，我将遍地上一切结种子的菜蔬和一切树上所结有核的果子全赐给你们作食物。
chapters.en: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.

chapters.zh: 至于地上的走兽和空中的飞鸟，并各样爬在地上有生命的物，我将青草赐给他们作食物。事就这样成了。
chapters.en: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein life, every green herb for meat: and it was so.

chapters.zh: 神看著一切所造的都甚好。有晚上，有早晨，是第六日。
chapters.en: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.

chapters.zh: 天地万物都造齐了。
chapters.en: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.

chapters.zh: 到第七日，神造物的工已经完毕，就在第七日歇了他一切的工，安息了。
chapters.en: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.

chapters.zh: 神赐福给第七日，定为圣日；因为在这日，神歇了他一切创造的工，就安息了。
chapters.en: And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.

chapters.zh: 创造天地的来历，在耶和华神造天地的日子，乃是这样，
chapters.en: These the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens,

chapters.zh: 野地还没有草木，田间的菜蔬还没有长起来；因为耶和华神还没有降雨在地上，也没有人耕地，
chapters.en: And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and not a man to till the ground.

chapters.zh: 但有雾气从地上腾，滋润遍地。
chapters.en: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神用地上的尘土造人，将生气吹在他鼻孔里，他就成了有灵的活人，名叫亚当。
chapters.en: And the LORD God formed man the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神在东方的伊甸立了一个园子，把所造的人安置在那里。
chapters.en: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神使各样的树从地里长出来，可以悦人的眼目，其上的果子好作食物。园子当中又有生命树和分别善恶的树。
chapters.en: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

chapters.zh: 有河从伊甸流出来，滋润那园子，从那里分为四道：
chapters.en: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.

chapters.zh: 第一道名叫比逊，就是环绕哈腓拉全地的。在那里有金子，
chapters.en: The name of the first Pison: that it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where gold;

chapters.zh: 并且那地的金子是好的；在那里又有珍珠和红玛瑙。
chapters.en: And the gold of that land good: there bdellium and the onyx stone.

chapters.zh: 第二道河名叫基训，就是环绕古实全地的。
chapters.en: And the name of the second river Gihon: the same it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.

chapters.zh: 第三道河名叫底格里斯，流在亚述的东边。第四道河就是伯拉河。
chapters.en: And the name of the third river Hiddekel: that it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river Euphrates.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神将那人安置在伊甸园，使他修理，看守。
chapters.en: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神吩咐他说：园中各样树上的果子，你可以随意吃，
chapters.en: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:

chapters.zh: 只是分别善恶树上的果子，你不可吃，因为你吃的日子必定死！
chapters.en: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神说：那人独居不好，我要为他造一个配偶帮助他。
chapters.en: And the LORD God said, not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神用土所造成的野地各样走兽和空中各样飞鸟都带到那人面前，看他叫甚么。那人怎样叫各样的活物，那就是他的名字。
chapters.en: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that the name thereof.

chapters.zh: 那人便给一切牲畜和空中飞鸟、野地走兽都起了名；只是那人没有遇见配偶帮助他。
chapters.en: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神使他沉睡，他就睡了；于是取下他的一条肋骨，又把肉合起来。
chapters.en: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;

chapters.zh: 耶和华神就用那人身上所取的肋骨造成一个女人，领他到那人跟前。
chapters.en: And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.

chapters.zh: 那人说：这是我骨中的骨，肉中的肉，可以称他为女人，因为他是从男人身上取出来的。
chapters.en: And Adam said, This now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.

chapters.zh: 因此，人要离开父母，与妻子连合，二人成为一体。
chapters.en: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.

chapters.zh: 当时夫妻二人赤身露体，并不羞耻。
chapters.en: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神所造的，惟有蛇比田野一切的活物更狡猾。蛇对女人说：神岂是真说不许你们吃园中所有树上的果子么？
chapters.en: Now the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the LORD God had made. And he said unto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?

chapters.zh: 女人对蛇说：园中树上的果子，我们可以吃，
chapters.en: And the woman said unto the serpent, We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden:

chapters.zh: 惟有园当中那棵树上的果子，神曾说：你们不可吃，也不可摸，免得你们死。
chapters.en: But of the fruit of the tree which in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.

chapters.zh: 蛇对女人说：你们不一定死；
chapters.en: And the serpent said unto the woman, Ye shall not surely die:

chapters.zh: 因为神知道，你们吃的日子眼睛就明亮了，你们便如神能知道善恶。
chapters.en: For God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.

chapters.zh: 于是女人见那棵树的果子好作食物，也悦人的眼目，且是可喜爱的，能使人有智慧，就摘下果子来吃了，又给他丈夫，他丈夫也吃了。
chapters.en: And when the woman saw that the tree good for food, and that it pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.

chapters.zh: 他们二人的眼睛就明亮了，才知道自己是赤身露体，便拿无花果树的叶子为自己编作裙子。
chapters.en: And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they naked; and they sewed fig leaves together, and made themselves aprons.

chapters.zh: 天起了凉风，耶和华神在园中行走。那人和他妻子听见神的声音，就藏在园里的树木中，躲避耶和华神的面。
chapters.en: And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神呼唤那人，对他说：你在那里？
chapters.en: And the LORD God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where thou?

chapters.zh: 他说：我在园中听见你的声音，我就害怕；因为我赤身露体，我便藏了。
chapters.en: And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I naked; and I hid myself.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：谁告诉你赤身露体呢？莫非你吃了我吩咐你不可吃的那树上的果子吗？
chapters.en: And he said, Who told thee that thou naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?

chapters.zh: 那人说：你所赐给我与我同居的女人，他把那树上的果子给我，我就吃了。
chapters.en: And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神对女人说：你作的是甚么事呢？女人说：那蛇引诱我，我就吃了。
chapters.en: And the LORD God said unto the woman, What this thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神对蛇说：你既作了这事，就必受咒诅，比一切的牲畜野兽更甚。你必用肚子行走，终身吃土。
chapters.en: And the LORD God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:

chapters.zh: 我又要叫你和女人彼此为仇；你的后裔和女人的后裔也彼此为仇。女人的后裔要伤你的头；你要伤他的脚跟。
chapters.en: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.

chapters.zh: 又对女人说：我必多多加增你怀胎的苦楚；你生产儿女必多受苦楚。你必恋慕你丈夫；你丈夫必管辖你。
chapters.en: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.

chapters.zh: 又对亚当说：你既听从妻子的话，吃了我所吩咐你不可吃的那树上的果子，地必为你的缘故受咒诅；你必终身劳苦才能从地里得吃的。
chapters.en: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat it all the days of thy life;

chapters.zh: 地必给你长出荆棘和蒺藜来；你也要吃田间的菜蔬。
chapters.en: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;

chapters.zh: 你必汗流满面才得糊口，直到你归了土，因为你是从土而出的。你本是尘土，仍要归于尘土。
chapters.en: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou , and unto dust shalt thou return.

chapters.zh: 亚当给他妻子起名叫夏娃，因为他是众生之母。
chapters.en: And Adam called his wife's name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神为亚当和他妻子用皮子作衣服给他们穿。
chapters.en: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the LORD God make coats of skins, and clothed them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华神说：那人已经与我们相似，能知道善恶；现在恐怕他伸手又摘生命树的果子吃，就永远活著。
chapters.en: And the LORD God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:

chapters.zh: 耶和华神便打发他出伊甸园去，耕种他所自出之土。
chapters.en: Therefore the LORD God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.

chapters.zh: 于是把他赶出去了；又在伊甸园的东边安设基路伯和四面转动发火燄的剑，要把守生命树的道路。
chapters.en: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.

chapters.zh: 有一日，那人和他妻子夏娃同房，夏娃就怀孕，生了该隐 ( 就是得的意思 )，便说：耶和华使我得了一个男子。
chapters.en: And Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 又生了该隐的兄弟亚伯。亚伯是牧羊的；该隐是种地的。
chapters.en: And she again bare his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.

chapters.zh: 有一日，该隐拿地里的出产为供物献给耶和华
chapters.en: And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚伯也将他羊群中头生的和羊的脂油献上。耶和华看中了亚伯和他的供物，
chapters.en: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the LORD had respect unto Abel and to his offering:

chapters.zh: 只是看不中该隐和他的供物。该隐就大大的发怒，变了脸色。
chapters.en: But unto Cain and to his offering he had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对该隐说：你为甚么发怒呢？你为甚么变了脸色呢？
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Cain, Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?

chapters.zh: 你若行得好，岂不蒙悦纳？你若行得不好，罪就伏在门前。他必恋慕你，你却要制伏他。
chapters.en: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.

chapters.zh: 该隐与他兄弟亚伯说话；二人正在田间。该隐起来打他兄弟亚伯，把他杀了。
chapters.en: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对该隐说：你兄弟亚伯在那里？他说：我不知道！我岂是看守我兄弟的么？
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Cain, Where Abel thy brother? And he said, I know not: I my brother's keeper?

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你作了甚么事呢？你兄弟的血有声音从地里向我哀告。
chapters.en: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto me from the ground.

chapters.zh: 地开了口，从你手里接受你兄弟的血。现在你必从这地受咒诅。
chapters.en: And now thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand;

chapters.zh: 你种地，地不再给你效力；你必流离飘荡在地上。
chapters.en: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.

chapters.zh: 该隐对耶和华说：我的刑罚太重，过于我所能当的。
chapters.en: And Cain said unto the LORD, My punishment greater than I can bear.

chapters.zh: 你如今赶逐我离开这地，以致不见你面；我必流离飘荡在地上，凡遇见我的必杀我。
chapters.en: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, every one that findeth me shall slay me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对他说：凡杀该隐的，必遭报七倍。耶和华就给该隐立一个记号，免得人遇见他就杀他。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the LORD set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.

chapters.zh: 于是该隐离开耶和华的面，去住在伊甸东边挪得之地。
chapters.en: And Cain went out from the presence of the LORD, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.

chapters.zh: 该隐与妻子同房，他妻子就怀孕，生了以诺。该隐建造了一座城，就按著他儿子的名将那城叫作以诺。
chapters.en: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bare Enoch: and he builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.

chapters.zh: 以诺生以拿；以拿生米户雅利；米户雅利生玛土撒利；玛土撒利生拉麦。
chapters.en: And unto Enoch was born Irad: and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.

chapters.zh: 拉麦娶了两个妻：一个名叫亚大，一个名叫洗拉。
chapters.en: And Lamech took unto him two wives: the name of the one Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.

chapters.zh: 亚大生雅八；雅八就是住帐棚、牧养牲畜之人的祖师。
chapters.en: And Adah bare Jabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and cattle.

chapters.zh: 雅八的兄弟名叫犹八；他是一切弹琴吹箫之人的祖师。
chapters.en: And his brother's name Jubal: he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ.

chapters.zh: 洗拉又生了土八该隐；他是打造各样铜铁利器的 ( 或作：是铜匠铁匠的祖师 )。土八该隐的妹子是拿玛。
chapters.en: And Zillah, she also bare Tubalcain, an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron: and the sister of Tubalcain Naamah.

chapters.zh: 拉麦对他两个妻子说：亚大、洗拉，听我的声音；拉麦的妻子，细听我的话语：壮年人伤我，我把他杀了；少年人损我，我把他害了 ( 或作我杀壮士却伤自己，我害幼童却损本身。)
chapters.en: And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.

chapters.zh: 若杀该隐，遭报七倍，杀拉麦，必遭报七十七倍。
chapters.en: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.

chapters.zh: 亚当又与妻子同房，他就生了一个儿子，起名叫塞特，意思说：神另给我立了一个儿子代替亚伯，因为该隐杀了他。
chapters.en: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, , hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.

chapters.zh: 塞特也生了一个儿子，起名叫以挪士。那时候，人才求告耶和华的名。
chapters.en: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚当的后代记在下面。〈 当神造人的日子，是照著自己的样式造的，
chapters.en: This the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;

chapters.zh: 并且造男造女。在他们被造的日子，神赐福给他们，称他们为人。〉
chapters.en: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.

chapters.zh: 亚当活到一百三十岁，生了一个儿子，形像样式和自己相似，就给他起名叫塞特。
chapters.en: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat in his own likeness, after his image; and called his name Seth:

chapters.zh: 亚当生塞特之后，又在世八百年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 亚当共活了九百三十岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 塞特活到一百零五岁，生了以挪士。
chapters.en: And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:

chapters.zh: 塞特生以挪士之后，又活了八百零七年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 塞特共活了九百一十二岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 以挪士活到九十岁，生了该南。
chapters.en: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:

chapters.zh: 以挪士生该南之后，又活了八百一十五年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 以挪士共活了九百零五岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 该南活到七十岁，生了玛勒列。
chapters.en: And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel:

chapters.zh: 该南生玛勒列之后，又活了八百四十年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 该南共活了九百一十岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 玛勒列活到六十五岁，生了雅列。
chapters.en: And Mahalaleel lived sixty and five years, and begat Jared:

chapters.zh: 玛勒列生雅列之后，又活了八百三十年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Mahalaleel lived after he begat Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 玛勒列共活了八百九十五岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred ninety and five years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 雅列活到一百六十二岁，生了以诺。
chapters.en: And Jared lived an hundred sixty and two years, and he begat Enoch:

chapters.zh: 雅列生以诺之后，又活了八百年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 雅列共活了九百六十二岁就死了 .。
chapters.en: And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 以诺活到六十五岁，生了玛土撒拉。
chapters.en: And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:

chapters.zh: 以诺生玛土撒拉之后，与神同行三百年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 以诺共活了三百六十五岁。
chapters.en: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:

chapters.zh: 以诺与神同行，神将他取去，他就不在世了。
chapters.en: And Enoch walked with God: and he not; for God took him.

chapters.zh: 玛土撒拉活到一百八十七岁，生了拉麦。
chapters.en: And Methuselah lived an hundred eighty and seven years, and begat Lamech:

chapters.zh: 玛土撒拉生拉麦之后，又活了七百八十二年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Methuselah lived after he begat Lamech seven hundred eighty and two years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 玛土撒拉共活了九百六十九岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty and nine years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 拉麦活到一百八十二岁，生了一个儿子，
chapters.en: And Lamech lived an hundred eighty and two years, and begat a son:

chapters.zh: 给他起名叫挪亚，说：这个儿子必为我们的操作和手中的劳苦安慰我们；这操作劳苦是因为耶和华咒诅地。
chapters.en: And he called his name Noah, saying, This shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD hath cursed.

chapters.zh: 拉麦生挪亚之后，又活了五百九十五年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Lamech lived after he begat Noah five hundred ninety and five years, and begat sons and daughters:

chapters.zh: 拉麦共活了七百七十七岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy and seven years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 挪亚五百岁生了闪、含、雅弗。
chapters.en: And Noah was five hundred years old: and Noah begat Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

chapters.zh: 当人在世上多起来又生女儿的时候，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them,

chapters.zh: 神的儿子们看见人的女子美貌，就随意挑选，娶来为妻。
chapters.en: That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：人既属乎血气，我的灵就不永远住在他里面；然而他的日子还可到一百二十年。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

chapters.zh: 那时候有伟人在地上，后来神的儿子们和人的女子们交合生子；那就是上古英武有名的人。
chapters.en: There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare to them, the same mighty men which of old, men of renown.

chapters.zh: 耶和华见人在地上罪恶很大，终日所思想的尽都是恶，
chapters.en: And GOD saw that the wickedness of man great in the earth, and every imagination of the thoughts of his heart only evil continually.

chapters.zh: 耶和华就后悔造人在地上，心中忧伤。
chapters.en: And it repented the LORD that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我要将所造的人和走兽，并昆虫，以及空中的飞鸟，都从地上除灭，因为我造他们后悔了。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.

chapters.zh: 惟有挪亚在耶和华眼前蒙恩。
chapters.en: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 挪亚的后代记在下面。挪亚是个义人，在当时的世代是个完全人。挪亚与神同行。
chapters.en: These the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man perfect in his generations, Noah walked with God.

chapters.zh: 挪亚生了三个儿子，就是闪、含、雅弗。
chapters.en: And Noah begat three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

chapters.zh: 世界在神面前败坏，地上满了强暴 .。
chapters.en: The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.

chapters.zh: 神观看世界，见是败坏了；凡有血气的人在地上 . 都败坏了行为。
chapters.en: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 神就对挪亚说：凡有血气的人，他的尽头已经来到我面前；因为地上满了他们的强暴，我要把他们和地一并毁灭。
chapters.en: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.

chapters.zh: 你要用歌斐木造一只方舟，分一间一间地造，里外抹上松香。
chapters.en: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.

chapters.zh: 方舟的造法乃是这样：要长三百肘，宽五十肘，高三十肘。
chapters.en: And this which thou shalt make it : The length of the ark three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.

chapters.zh: 方舟上边要留透光处，高一肘。方舟的门要开在旁边。方舟要分上、中、下三层。
chapters.en: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; lower, second, and third shalt thou make it.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我要使洪水泛滥在地上，毁灭天下；凡地上有血肉、有气息的活物，无一不死。
chapters.en: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein the breath of life, from under heaven; every thing that in the earth shall die.

chapters.zh: 我却要与你立约；你同你的妻，与儿子儿妇，都要进入方舟。
chapters.en: But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons' wives with thee.

chapters.zh: 凡有血肉的活物，每样两个，一公一母，你要带进方舟，好在你那里保全生命。
chapters.en: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep alive with thee; they shall be male and female.

chapters.zh: 飞鸟各从其类，牲畜各从其类，地上的昆虫各从其类，每样两个，要到你那里，好保全生命。
chapters.en: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every shall come unto thee, to keep alive.

chapters.zh: 你要拿各样食物积蓄起来，好作你和他们的食物。
chapters.en: And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather to thee; and it shall be for food for thee, and for them.

chapters.zh: 挪亚就这样行。凡神所吩咐的，他都照样行了。
chapters.en: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对挪亚说：你和你的全家都要进入方舟；因为在这世代中，我见你在我面前是义人。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.

chapters.zh: 凡洁净的畜类，你要带七公七母；不洁净的畜类，你要带一公一母；
chapters.en: Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee by sevens, the male and his female: and of beasts that not clean by two, the male and his female.

chapters.zh: 空中的飞鸟也要带七公七母，可以留种，活在全地上；
chapters.en: Of fowls also of the air by sevens, the male and the female; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.

chapters.zh: 因为再过七天，我要降雨在地上四十昼夜，把我所造的各种活物都从地上除灭。
chapters.en: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living substance that I have made will I destroy from off the face of the earth.

chapters.zh: 挪亚就遵著耶和华所吩咐的行了。
chapters.en: And Noah did according unto all that the LORD commanded him.

chapters.zh: 当洪水泛滥在地上的时候，挪亚整六百岁。
chapters.en: And Noah six hundred years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 挪亚就同他的妻和儿子儿妇都进入方舟，躲避洪水。
chapters.en: And Noah went in, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him, into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.

chapters.zh: 洁净的畜类和不洁净的畜类，飞鸟并地上一切的昆虫，
chapters.en: Of clean beasts, and of beasts that not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,

chapters.zh: 都是一对一对的，有公有母，到挪亚那里进入方舟，正如神所吩咐挪亚的。
chapters.en: There went in two and two unto Noah into the ark, the male and the female, as God had commanded Noah.

chapters.zh: 过了那七天，洪水泛滥在地上。
chapters.en: And it came to pass after seven days, that the waters of the flood were upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 当挪亚六百岁，二月十七日那一天，大渊的泉源都裂开了，天上的窗户也敞开了
chapters.en: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, the seventeenth day of the month, the same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened.

chapters.zh: 四十昼夜降大雨在地上。
chapters.en: And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

chapters.zh: 正当那日，挪亚和他三个儿子闪、含、雅弗，并挪亚的妻子和三个儿妇，都进入方舟。
chapters.en: In the selfsame day entered Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth, the sons of Noah, and Noah's wife, and the three wives of his sons with them, into the ark;

chapters.zh: 他们和百兽，各从其类，一切牲畜，各从其类，爬在地上的昆虫，各从其类，一切禽鸟，各从其类，都进入方舟。
chapters.en: They, and every beast after his kind, and all the cattle after their kind, and every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind, and every fowl after his kind, every bird of every sort.

chapters.zh: 凡有血肉、有气息的活物，都一对一对地到挪亚那里，进入方舟。
chapters.en: And they went in unto Noah into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein the breath of life.

chapters.zh: 凡有血肉进入方舟的，都是有公有母，正如神所吩咐挪亚的。耶和华就把他关在方舟里头。
chapters.en: And they that went in, went in male and female of all flesh, as God had commanded him: and the LORD shut him in.

chapters.zh: 洪水泛滥在地上四十天，水往上长，把方舟从地上漂起。
chapters.en: And the flood was forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bare up the ark, and it was lift up above the earth.

chapters.zh: 水势浩大，在地上大大地往上长，方舟在水面上漂来漂去。
chapters.en: And the waters prevailed, and were increased greatly upon the earth; and the ark went upon the face of the waters.

chapters.zh: 水势在地上极其浩大，天下的高山都淹没了。
chapters.en: And the waters prevailed exceedingly upon the earth; and all the high hills, that under the whole heaven, were covered.

chapters.zh: 水势比山高过十五肘，山岭都淹没了。
chapters.en: Fifteen cubits upward did the waters prevail; and the mountains were covered.

chapters.zh: 凡在地上有血肉的动物，就是飞鸟、牲畜、走兽，和爬在地上的昆虫，以及所有的人，都死了。
chapters.en: And all flesh died that moved upon the earth, both of fowl, and of cattle, and of beast, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, and every man:

chapters.zh: 凡在旱地上、鼻孔有气息的生灵都死了。
chapters.en: All in whose nostrils the breath of life, of all that in the dry , died.

chapters.zh: 凡地上各类的活物，连人带牲畜、昆虫，以及空中的飞鸟，都从地上除灭了，只留下挪亚和那些与他同在方舟里的。
chapters.en: And every living substance was destroyed which was upon the face of the ground, both man, and cattle, and the creeping things, and the fowl of the heaven; and they were destroyed from the earth: and Noah only remained , and they that with him in the ark.

chapters.zh: 水势浩大，在地上共一百五十天。
chapters.en: And the waters prevailed upon the earth an hundred and fifty days.

chapters.zh: 神记念挪亚和挪亚方舟里的一切走兽牲畜。神叫风吹地，水势渐落。
chapters.en: And God remembered Noah, and every living thing, and all the cattle that with him in the ark: and God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters asswaged;

chapters.zh: 渊源和天上的窗户都闭塞了，天上的大雨也止住了。
chapters.en: The fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped, and the rain from heaven was restrained;

chapters.zh: 水从地上渐退。过了一百五十天，水就渐消。
chapters.en: And the waters returned from off the earth continually: and after the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters were abated.

chapters.zh: 七月十七日，方舟停在亚拉腊山上。
chapters.en: And the ark rested in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, upon the mountains of Ararat.

chapters.zh: 水又渐消，到十月初一日，山顶都现出来了。
chapters.en: And the waters decreased continually until the tenth month: in the tenth , on the first of the month, were the tops of the mountains seen.

chapters.zh: 过了四十天，挪亚开了方舟的窗户，
chapters.en: And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made:

chapters.zh: 放出一只乌鸦去；那乌鸦飞来飞去，直到地上的水都干了。
chapters.en: And he sent forth a raven, which went forth to and fro, until the waters were dried up from off the earth.

chapters.zh: 他又放出一只鸽子去，要看看水从地上退了没有。
chapters.en: Also he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground;

chapters.zh: 但遍地上都是水，鸽子找不著落脚之地，就回到方舟挪亚那里，挪亚伸手把鸽子接进方舟来。
chapters.en: But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she returned unto him into the ark, for the waters on the face of the whole earth: then he put forth his hand, and took her, and pulled her in unto him into the ark.

chapters.zh: 他又等了七天，再把鸽子从方舟放出去。
chapters.en: And he stayed yet other seven days; and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark;

chapters.zh: 到了晚上，鸽子回到他那里，嘴里叼著一个新拧下来的橄榄叶子，挪亚就知道地上的水退了。
chapters.en: And the dove came in to him in the evening; and, lo, in her mouth an olive leaf pluckt off: so Noah knew that the waters were abated from off the earth.

chapters.zh: 他又等了七天，放出鸽子去，鸽子就不再回来了。
chapters.en: And he stayed yet other seven days; and sent forth the dove; which returned not again unto him any more.

chapters.zh: 到挪亚六百零一岁，正月初一日，地上的水都干了。挪亚撤去方舟的盖观看，便见地面上干了。
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the six hundredth and first year, in the first , the first of the month, the waters were dried up from off the earth: and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and, behold, the face of the ground was dry.

chapters.zh: 到了二月二十七日，地就都干了。
chapters.en: And in the second month, on the seven and twentieth day of the month, was the earth dried.

chapters.zh: 神对挪亚说：
chapters.en: And God spake unto Noah, saying,

chapters.zh: 你和你的妻子、儿子、儿妇都可以出方舟。
chapters.en: Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons' wives with thee.

chapters.zh: 在你那里凡有血肉的活物，就是飞鸟、牲畜，和一切爬在地上的昆虫，都要带出来，叫他在地上多多滋生，大大兴旺。
chapters.en: Bring forth with thee every living thing that with thee, of all flesh, of fowl, and of cattle, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth; that they may breed abundantly in the earth, and be fruitful, and multiply upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 于是挪亚和他的妻子、儿子、儿妇都出来了。
chapters.en: And Noah went forth, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him:

chapters.zh: 一切走兽、昆虫、飞鸟，和地上所有的动物，各从其类，也都出了方舟。
chapters.en: Every beast, every creeping thing, and every fowl, whatsoever creepeth upon the earth, after their kinds, went forth out of the ark.

chapters.zh: 挪亚为耶和华筑了一座坛，拿各类洁净的牲畜、飞鸟献在坛上为燔祭。
chapters.en: And Noah builded an altar unto the LORD; and took of every clean beast, and of every clean fowl, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

chapters.zh: 耶和华闻那馨香之气，就心里说：我不再因人的缘故咒诅地（人从小时心里怀著恶念），也不再按著我才行的灭各种的活物了。
chapters.en: And the LORD smelled a sweet savour; and the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man's sake; for the imagination of man's heart evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.

chapters.zh: 地还存留的时候，稼穑、寒暑、冬夏、昼夜就永不停息了。
chapters.en: While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.

chapters.zh: 神赐福给挪亚和他的儿子，对他们说：你们要生养众多，遍满了地。
chapters.en: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.

chapters.zh: 凡地上的走兽和空中的飞鸟都必惊恐，惧怕你们，连地上一切的昆虫并海里一切的鱼都交付你们的手。
chapters.en: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every fowl of the air, upon all that moveth the earth, and upon all the fishes of the sea; into your hand are they delivered.

chapters.zh: 凡活著的动物都可以作你们的食物。这一切我都赐给你们，如同菜蔬一样。
chapters.en: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.

chapters.zh: 惟独肉带著血，那就是他的生命，你们不可吃。
chapters.en: But flesh with the life thereof, the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.

chapters.zh: 流你们血、害你们命的，无论是兽是人，我必讨他的罪，就是向各人的弟兄也是如此。
chapters.en: And surely your blood of your lives will I require; at the hand of every beast will I require it, and at the hand of man; at the hand of every man's brother will I require the life of man.

chapters.zh: 凡流人血的，他的血也必被人所流，因为神造人是照自己的形像造的。
chapters.en: Whoso sheddeth man's blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.

chapters.zh: 你们要生养众多，在地上昌盛繁茂。
chapters.en: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.

chapters.zh: 神晓谕挪亚和他的儿子说：
chapters.en: And God spake unto Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,

chapters.zh: 我与你们和你们的后裔立约，
chapters.en: And I, behold, I establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you;

chapters.zh: 并与你们这里的一切活物 ─ 就是飞鸟、牲畜、走兽，凡从方舟里出来的活物 ─ 立约。
chapters.en: And with every living creature that with you, of the fowl, of the cattle, and of every beast of the earth with you; from all that go out of the ark, to every beast of the earth.

chapters.zh: 我与你们立约，凡有血肉的，不再被洪水灭绝，也不再有洪水毁坏地了。
chapters.en: And I will establish my covenant with you; neither shall all flesh be cut off any more by the waters of a flood; neither shall there any more be a flood to destroy the earth.

chapters.zh: 神说：我与你们并你们这里的各样活物所立的永约是有记号的。
chapters.en: And God said, This the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that with you, for perpetual generations:

chapters.zh: 我把虹放在云彩中，这就可作我与地立约的记号了。
chapters.en: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.

chapters.zh: 我使云彩盖地的时候，必有虹现在云彩中，
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:

chapters.zh: 我便记念我与你们和各样有血肉的活物所立的约，水就再不泛滥、毁坏一切有血肉的物了。
chapters.en: And I will remember my covenant, which between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

chapters.zh: 虹必现在云彩中，我看见，就要记念我与地上各样有血肉的活物所立的永约。
chapters.en: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 神对挪亚说：这就是我与地上一切有血肉之物立约的记号了。
chapters.en: And God said unto Noah, This the token of the covenant, which I have established between me and all flesh that upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 出方舟挪亚的儿子就是闪、含、雅弗。含是迦南的父亲。
chapters.en: And the sons of Noah, that went forth of the ark, were Shem, and Ham, and Japheth: and Ham the father of Canaan.

chapters.zh: 这是挪亚的三个儿子，他们的后裔分散在全地。
chapters.en: These the three sons of Noah: and of them was the whole earth overspread.

chapters.zh: 挪亚作起农夫来，栽了一个葡萄园。
chapters.en: And Noah began an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard:

chapters.zh: 他喝了园中的酒便醉了，在帐棚里赤著身子。
chapters.en: And he drank of the wine, and was drunken; and he was uncovered within his tent.

chapters.zh: 迦南的父亲含看见他父亲赤身，就到外边告诉他两个弟兄。
chapters.en: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brethren without.

chapters.zh: 于是闪和雅弗拿件衣服搭在肩上，倒退著进去，给他父亲盖上；他们背著脸就看不见父亲的赤身。
chapters.en: And Shem and Japheth took a garment, and laid upon both their shoulders, and went backward, and covered the nakedness of their father; and their faces backward, and they saw not their father's nakedness.

chapters.zh: 挪亚醒了酒，知道小儿子向他所作的事，
chapters.en: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his younger son had done unto him.

chapters.zh: 就说：迦南当受咒诅，必给他弟兄作奴仆的奴仆；
chapters.en: And he said, Cursed Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.

chapters.zh: 又说：耶和华 ─ 闪的神是应当称颂的！愿迦南作闪的奴仆。
chapters.en: And he said, Blessed the LORD God of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

chapters.zh: 愿神使雅弗扩张，使他住在闪的帐棚里；又愿迦南作他的奴仆。
chapters.en: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

chapters.zh: 洪水以后，挪亚又活了三百五十年。
chapters.en: And Noah lived after the flood three hundred and fifty years.

chapters.zh: 挪亚共活了九百五十岁就死了。
chapters.en: And all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years: and he died.

chapters.zh: 挪亚的儿子闪、含、雅弗的后代记在下面。洪水以后，他们都生了儿子。
chapters.en: Now these the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth: and unto them were sons born after the flood.

chapters.zh: 雅弗的儿子是歌篾、玛各、玛代、雅完、土巴、米设、提拉。
chapters.en: The sons of Japheth; Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech, and Tiras.

chapters.zh: 歌篾的儿子是亚实基拿、利法、陀迦玛。
chapters.en: And the sons of Gomer; Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.

chapters.zh: 雅完的儿子是以利沙、他施、基提、多单。
chapters.en: And the sons of Javan; Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

chapters.zh: 这些人的后裔将各国的地土、海岛分开居住，各随各的方言、宗族立国。
chapters.en: By these were the isles of the Gentiles divided in their lands; every one after his tongue, after their families, in their nations.

chapters.zh: 含的儿子是古实、麦西、弗、迦南。
chapters.en: And the sons of Ham; Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.

chapters.zh: 古实的儿子是西巴、哈腓拉、撒弗他、拉玛、撒弗提迦。拉玛的儿子是示巴、底但。
chapters.en: And the sons of Cush; Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtecha: and the sons of Raamah; Sheba, and Dedan.

chapters.zh: 古实又生宁录，他为世上英雄之首。
chapters.en: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.

chapters.zh: 他在耶和华面前是个英勇的猎户，所以俗语说：像宁录在耶和华面前是个英勇的猎户。
chapters.en: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD: wherefore it is said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他国的起头是巴别、以力、亚甲、甲尼，都在示拿地。
chapters.en: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

chapters.zh: 他从那地出来往亚述去，建造尼尼微、利河伯、迦拉，
chapters.en: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineveh, and the city Rehoboth, and Calah,

chapters.zh: 和尼尼微、迦拉中间的利鲜，这就是那大城。
chapters.en: And Resen between Nineveh and Calah: the same a great city.

chapters.zh: 麦西生路低人、亚拿米人、利哈比人、拿弗土希人、
chapters.en: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,

chapters.zh: 帕斯鲁细人、迦斯路希人、迦斐托人；从迦斐托出来的有非利士人。
chapters.en: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim, (out of whom came Philistim,) and Caphtorim.

chapters.zh: 迦南生长子西顿，又生赫
chapters.en: And Canaan begat Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,

chapters.zh: 和耶布斯人、亚摩利人、革迦撒人、
chapters.en: And the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgasite,

chapters.zh: 希未人、亚基人、西尼人、
chapters.en: And the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,

chapters.zh: 亚瓦底人、洗玛利人、哈马人，后来迦南的诸族分散了。
chapters.en: And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.

chapters.zh: 迦南的境界是从西顿向基拉耳的路上，直到迦萨，又向所多玛、蛾摩拉、押玛、洗扁的路上，直到拉沙。
chapters.en: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon, as thou comest to Gerar, unto Gaza; as thou goest, unto Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Admah, and Zeboim, even unto Lasha.

chapters.zh: 这就是含的后裔，各随他们的宗族、方言，所住的地土、邦国。
chapters.en: These the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their countries, in their nations.

chapters.zh: 雅弗的哥哥闪，是希伯子孙之祖，他也生了儿子。
chapters.en: Unto Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder, even to him were born.

chapters.zh: 闪的儿子是以拦、亚述、亚法撒、路德、亚兰。
chapters.en: The children of Shem; Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.

chapters.zh: 亚兰的儿子是乌斯、户勒、基帖、玛施。
chapters.en: And the children of Aram; Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.

chapters.zh: 亚法撒生沙拉；沙拉生希伯。
chapters.en: And Arphaxad begat Salah; and Salah begat Eber.

chapters.zh: 希伯生了两个儿子，一个名叫法勒 ( 法勒就是分的意思 )，因为那时人就分地居住；法勒的兄弟名叫约坍。
chapters.en: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided; and his brother's name Joktan.

chapters.zh: 约坍生亚摩答、沙列、哈萨玛非、耶拉、
chapters.en: And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,

chapters.zh: 哈多兰、乌萨、德拉、
chapters.en: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,

chapters.zh: 俄巴路、亚比玛利、示巴、
chapters.en: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,

chapters.zh: 阿斐、哈腓拉、约巴，这都是约坍的儿子。
chapters.en: And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these the sons of Joktan.

chapters.zh: 他们所住的地方是从米沙直到西发东边的山。
chapters.en: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest unto Sephar a mount of the east.

chapters.zh: 这就是闪的子孙，各随他们的宗族、方言，所住的地土、邦国。
chapters.en: These the sons of Shem, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands, after their nations.

chapters.zh: 这些都是挪亚三个儿子的宗族，各随他们的支派立国。洪水以后，他们在地上分为邦国。
chapters.en: These the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations divided in the earth after the flood.

chapters.zh: 那时，天下人的口音、言语都是一样。
chapters.en: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.

chapters.zh: 他们往东边迁移的时候，在示拿地遇见一片平原，就住在那里。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.

chapters.zh: 他们彼此商量说：来罢！我们要作砖，把砖烧透了。他们就拿砖当石头，又拿石漆当灰泥。
chapters.en: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter.

chapters.zh: 他们说：来罢！我们要建造一座城和一座塔，塔顶通天，为要传扬我们的名，免得我们分散在全地上。
chapters.en: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华降临，要看看世人所建造的城和塔。
chapters.en: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：看哪，他们成为一样的人民，都是一样的言语，如今既作起这事来，以后他们所要作的事就没有不成就的了。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, Behold, the people one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.

chapters.zh: 我们下去，在那里变乱他们的口音，使他们的言语彼此不通。
chapters.en: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another's speech.

chapters.zh: 于是耶和华使他们从那里分散在全地上；他们就停工，不造那城了。
chapters.en: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华在那里变乱天下人的言语，使众人分散在全地上，所以那城名叫巴别 ( 就是变乱的意思 )。
chapters.en: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the LORD scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth.

chapters.zh: 闪的后代记在下面。洪水以后二年，闪一百岁生了亚法撒。
chapters.en: These the generations of Shem: Shem an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:

chapters.zh: 闪生亚法撒之后又活了五百年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 亚法撒活到三十五岁，生了沙拉。
chapters.en: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:

chapters.zh: 亚法撒生沙拉之后又活了四百零三年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 沙拉活到三十岁，生了希伯。
chapters.en: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:

chapters.zh: 沙拉生希伯之后又活了四百零三年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 希伯活到三十四岁，生了法勒。
chapters.en: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:

chapters.zh: 希伯生法勒之后又活了四百三十年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 法勒活到三十岁，生了拉吴。
chapters.en: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:

chapters.zh: 法勒生拉吴之后又活了二百零九年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 拉吴活到三十二岁，生了西鹿。
chapters.en: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:

chapters.zh: 拉吴生西鹿之后又活了二百零七年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 西鹿活到三十岁，生了拿鹤。
chapters.en: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:

chapters.zh: 西鹿生拿鹤之后又活了二百年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 拿鹤活到二十九岁，生了他拉。
chapters.en: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:

chapters.zh: 拿鹤生他拉之后又活了一百一十九年，并且生儿养女。
chapters.en: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.

chapters.zh: 他拉活到七十岁，生了亚伯兰、拿鹤、哈兰。
chapters.en: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

chapters.zh: 他拉的后代记在下面。他拉生亚伯兰、拿鹤、哈兰；哈兰生罗得。
chapters.en: Now these the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran begat Lot.

chapters.zh: 哈兰死在他的本地迦勒底的吾珥，在他父亲他拉之先。
chapters.en: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰、拿鹤各娶了妻：亚伯兰的妻子名叫撒莱；拿鹤的妻子名叫密迦，是哈兰的女儿；哈兰是密迦和亦迦的父亲。
chapters.en: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram's wife Sarai; and the name of Nahor's wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.

chapters.zh: 撒莱不生育，没有孩子。
chapters.en: But Sarai was barren; she no child.

chapters.zh: 他拉带著他儿子亚伯兰和他孙子哈兰的儿子罗得，并他儿妇亚伯兰的妻子撒莱，出了迦勒底的吾珥，要往迦南地去；他们走到哈兰，就住在那里。
chapters.en: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son's son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram's wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.

chapters.zh: 他拉共活了二百零五岁，就死在哈兰。
chapters.en: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对亚伯兰说：你要离开本地、本族、父家，往我所要指示你的地去。
chapters.en: Now the LORD had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father's house, unto a land that I will shew thee:

chapters.zh: 我必叫你成为大国。我必赐福给你，叫你的名为大；你也要叫别人得福。
chapters.en: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:

chapters.zh: 为你祝福的，我必赐福与他；那咒诅你的，我必咒诅他。地上的万族都要因你得福。
chapters.en: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰就照著耶和华的吩咐去了；罗得也和他同去。亚伯兰出哈兰的时候年七十五岁。
chapters.en: So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰将他妻子撒莱和姪儿罗得，连他们在哈兰所积蓄的财物、所得的人口，都带往迦南地去。他们就到了迦南地。
chapters.en: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the souls that they had gotten in Haran; and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan; and into the land of Canaan they came.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰经过那地，到了示剑地方、摩利橡树那里。那时迦南人住在那地。
chapters.en: And Abram passed through the land unto the place of Sichem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanite then in the land.

chapters.zh: 耶和华向亚伯兰显现，说：我要把这地赐给你的后裔。亚伯兰就在那里为向他显现的耶和华筑了一座坛。
chapters.en: And the LORD appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded he an altar unto the LORD, who appeared unto him.

chapters.zh: 从那里他又迁到伯特利东边的山，支搭帐棚；西边是伯特利，东边是艾。他在那里又为耶和华筑了一座坛，求告耶和华的名。
chapters.en: And he removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east: and there he builded an altar unto the LORD, and called upon the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 后来亚伯兰又渐渐迁往南地去。
chapters.en: And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.

chapters.zh: 那地遭遇饥荒。因饥荒甚大，亚伯兰就下埃及去，要在那里暂居。
chapters.en: And there was a famine in the land: and Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there; for the famine grievous in the land.

chapters.zh: 将近埃及，就对他妻子撒莱说：我知道你是容貌俊美的妇人。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when he was come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife, Behold now, I know that thou a fair woman to look upon:

chapters.zh: 埃及人看见你必说：这是他的妻子，他们就要杀我，却叫你存活。
chapters.en: Therefore it shall come to pass, when the Egyptians shall see thee, that they shall say, This his wife: and they will kill me, but they will save thee alive.

chapters.zh: 求你说，你是我的妹子，使我因你得平安，我的命也因你存活。
chapters.en: Say, I pray thee, thou my sister: that it may be well with me for thy sake; and my soul shall live because of thee.

chapters.zh: 及至亚伯兰到了埃及，埃及人看见那妇人极其美貌。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that, when Abram was come into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman that she very fair.

chapters.zh: 法老的臣宰看见了他，就在法老面前夸奖他。那妇人就被带进法老的宫去。
chapters.en: The princes also of Pharaoh saw her, and commended her before Pharaoh: and the woman was taken into Pharaoh's house.

chapters.zh: 法老因这妇人就厚待亚伯兰，亚伯兰得了许多牛、羊、骆驼、公驴、母驴、仆婢。
chapters.en: And he entreated Abram well for her sake: and he had sheep, and oxen, and he asses, and menservants, and maidservants, and she asses, and camels.

chapters.zh: 耶和华因亚伯兰妻子撒莱的缘故，降大灾与法老和他的全家。
chapters.en: And the LORD plagued Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai Abram's wife.

chapters.zh: 法老就召了亚伯兰来，说：你这向我作的是甚么事呢？为甚么没有告诉我他是你的妻子？
chapters.en: And Pharaoh called Abram, and said, What this thou hast done unto me? why didst thou not tell me that she thy wife?

chapters.zh: 为甚么说他是你的妹子，以致我把他取来要作我的妻子？现在你的妻子在这里，可以带他走罢。
chapters.en: Why saidst thou, She my sister? so I might have taken her to me to wife: now therefore behold thy wife, take , and go thy way.

chapters.zh: 于是法老吩咐人将亚伯兰和他妻子，并他所有的都送走了。
chapters.en: And Pharaoh commanded men concerning him: and they sent him away, and his wife, and all that he had.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰带著他的妻子与罗得，并一切所有的，都从埃及上南地去。
chapters.en: And Abram went up out of Egypt, he, and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the south.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰的金、银、牲畜极多。
chapters.en: And Abram very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.

chapters.zh: 他从南地渐渐往伯特利去，到了伯特利和艾的中间，就是从前支搭帐棚的地方，
chapters.en: And he went on his journeys from the south even to Bethel, unto the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Hai;

chapters.zh: 也是他起先筑坛的地方；他又在那里求告耶和华的名。
chapters.en: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 与亚伯兰同行的罗得也有牛群、羊群、帐棚。
chapters.en: And Lot also, which went with Abram, had flocks, and herds, and tents.

chapters.zh: 那地容不下他们；因为他们的财物甚多，使他们不能同居。
chapters.en: And the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great, so that they could not dwell together.

chapters.zh: 当时，迦南人与比利洗人在那地居住。亚伯兰的牧人和罗得的牧人相争。
chapters.en: And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram's cattle and the herdmen of Lot's cattle: and the Canaanite and the Perizzite dwelled then in the land.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰就对罗得说：你我不可相争，你的牧人和我的牧人也不可相争，因为我们是骨肉 ( 原文作弟兄 )。
chapters.en: And Abram said unto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee, and between my herdmen and thy herdmen; for we brethren.

chapters.zh: 遍地不都在你眼前么？请你离开我：你向左，我就向右；你向右，我就向左。
chapters.en: not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me: if the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if to the right hand, then I will go to the left.

chapters.zh: 罗得举目看见约但河的全平原，直到琐珥，都是滋润的，那地在耶和华未灭所多玛、蛾摩拉以先如同耶和华的园子，也像埃及地。
chapters.en: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and beheld all the plain of Jordan, that it well watered every where, before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, as the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as thou comest unto Zoar.

chapters.zh: 于是罗得选择约但河的全平原，往东迁移；他们就彼此分离了。
chapters.en: Then Lot chose him all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰住在迦南地，罗得住在平原的城邑，渐渐挪移帐棚，直到所多玛。
chapters.en: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plain, and pitched tent toward Sodom.

chapters.zh: 所多玛人在耶和华面前罪大恶极。
chapters.en: But the men of Sodom wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly.

chapters.zh: 罗得离别亚伯兰以后，耶和华对亚伯兰说：从你所在的地方，你举目向东西南北观看；
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward:

chapters.zh: 凡你所看见的一切地，我都要赐给你和你的后裔，直到永远。
chapters.en: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed for ever.

chapters.zh: 我也要使你的后裔如同地上的尘沙那样多，人若能数算地上的尘沙才能数算你的后裔。
chapters.en: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, shall thy seed also be numbered.

chapters.zh: 你起来，纵横走遍这地，因为我必把这地赐给你。
chapters.en: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰就搬了帐棚，来到希伯仑幔利的橡树那里居住，在那里为耶和华筑了一座坛。
chapters.en: Then Abram removed tent, and came and dwelt in the plain of Mamre, which in Hebron, and built there an altar unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 当暗拉非作示拿王，亚略作以拉撒王，基大老玛作以拦王，提达作戈印王的时候，
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations;

chapters.zh: 他们都攻打所多玛王比拉、蛾摩拉王比沙、押玛王示纳、洗扁王善以别，和比拉王；比拉就是琐珥。
chapters.en: made war with Bera king of Sodom, and with Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, and Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, which is Zoar.

chapters.zh: 这五王都在西订谷会合；西订谷就是盐海。
chapters.en: All these were joined together in the vale of Siddim, which is the salt sea.

chapters.zh: 他们已经事奉基大老玛十二年，到十三年就背叛了。
chapters.en: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

chapters.zh: 十四年，基大老玛和同盟的王都来在亚特律加宁，杀败了利乏音人，在哈麦杀败了苏西人，在沙微基列亭杀败了以米人，
chapters.en: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer, and the kings that with him, and smote the Rephaims in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaveh Kiriathaim,

chapters.zh: 在何利人的西珥山杀败了何利人，一直杀到靠近旷野的伊勒巴兰。
chapters.en: And the Horites in their mount Seir, unto Elparan, which by the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 他们回到安密巴，就是加低斯，杀败了亚玛力全地的人，以及住在哈洗逊他玛的亚摩利人。
chapters.en: And they returned, and came to Enmishpat, which Kadesh, and smote all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites, that dwelt in Hazezontamar.

chapters.zh: 于是所多玛王、蛾摩拉王、押玛王、洗扁王，和比拉王（比拉就是琐珥）都出来，在西订谷摆阵，与他们交战，
chapters.en: And there went out the king of Sodom, and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah, and the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (the same Zoar;) and they joined battle with them in the vale of Siddim;

chapters.zh: 就是与以拦王基大老玛、戈印王提达、示拿王暗拉非、以拉撒王亚略交战；乃是四王与五王交战。
chapters.en: With Chedorlaomer the king of Elam, and with Tidal king of nations, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar; four kings with five.

chapters.zh: 西订谷有许多石漆坑。所多玛王和蛾摩拉王逃跑，有掉在坑里的，其余的人都往山上逃跑。
chapters.en: And the vale of Siddim slimepits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there; and they that remained fled to the mountain.

chapters.zh: 四王就把所多玛和蛾摩拉所有的财物，并一切的粮食都掳掠去了；
chapters.en: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.

chapters.zh: 又把亚伯兰的姪儿罗得和罗得的财物掳掠去了。当时罗得正住在所多玛。
chapters.en: And they took Lot, Abram's brother's son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.

chapters.zh: 有一个逃出来的人告诉希伯来人亚伯兰；亚伯兰正住在亚摩利人幔利的橡树那里。幔利和以实各并亚乃都是弟兄，曾与亚伯兰联盟。
chapters.en: And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew; for he dwelt in the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these confederate with Abram.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰听见他姪儿 ( 原文作弟兄 ) 被掳去，就率领他家里生养的精练壮丁三百一十八人，直追到但，
chapters.en: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained , born in his own house, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued unto Dan.

chapters.zh: 便在夜间，自己同仆人分队杀败敌人，又追到大马色左边的何把，
chapters.en: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them, and pursued them unto Hobah, which on the left hand of Damascus.

chapters.zh: 将被掳掠的一切财物夺回来，连他姪儿罗得和他的财物，以及妇女、人民也都夺回来。
chapters.en: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought again his brother Lot, and his goods, and the women also, and the people.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰杀败基大老玛和与他同盟的王回来的时候，所多玛王出来，在沙微谷迎接他；沙微谷就是王谷。
chapters.en: And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer, and of the kings that with him, at the valley of Shaveh, which the king's dale.

chapters.zh: 又有撒冷王麦基洗德带著饼和酒出来迎接；他是至高神的祭司。
chapters.en: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he the priest of the most high God.

chapters.zh: 他为亚伯兰祝福，说：愿天地的主、至高的神赐福与亚伯兰！
chapters.en: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:

chapters.zh: 至高的神把敌人交在你手里，是应当称颂的！亚伯兰就把所得的拿出十分之一来，给麦基洗德。
chapters.en: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. And he gave him tithes of all.

chapters.zh: 所多玛王对亚伯兰说：你把人口给我，财物你自己拿去罢！
chapters.en: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰对所多玛王说：我已经向天地的主 ─ 至高的神耶和华起誓：
chapters.en: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the LORD, the most high God, the possessor of heaven and earth,

chapters.zh: 凡是你的东西，就是一根线、一根鞋带，我都不拿，免得你说：我使亚伯兰富足！
chapters.en: That I will not from a thread even to a shoelatchet, and that I will not take any thing that thine, lest thou shouldest say, I have made Abram rich:

chapters.zh: 只有仆人所吃的，并与我同行的亚乃、以实各、幔利所应得的分，可以任凭他们拿去。
chapters.en: Save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men which went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their portion.

chapters.zh: 这事以后，耶和华在异象中有话对亚伯兰说：亚伯兰，你不要惧怕！我是你的盾牌，必大大的赏赐你。
chapters.en: After these things the word of the LORD came unto Abram in a vision, saying, Fear not, Abram: I thy shield, thy exceeding great reward.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰说：主耶和华阿，我既无子，你还赐我甚么呢？并且要承受我家业的是大马色人以利以谢。
chapters.en: And Abram said, Lord GOD, what wilt thou give me, seeing I go childless, and the steward of my house this Eliezer of Damascus?

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰又说：你没有给我儿子；那生在我家中的人就是我的后嗣。
chapters.en: And Abram said, Behold, to me thou hast given no seed: and, lo, one born in my house is mine heir.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又有话对他说：这人必不成为你的后嗣；你本身所生的才成为你的后嗣。
chapters.en: And, behold, the word of the LORD unto him, saying, This shall not be thine heir; but he that shall come forth out of thine own bowels shall be thine heir.

chapters.zh: 于是领他走到外边，说：你向天观看，数算众星，能数得过来么？又对他说：你的后裔将要如此。
chapters.en: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars, if thou be able to number them: and he said unto him, So shall thy seed be.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰信耶和华，耶和华就以此为他的义。
chapters.en: And he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又对他说：我是耶和华，曾领你出了迦勒底的吾珥，为要将这地赐你为业。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, I the LORD that brought thee out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰说：主耶和华阿，我怎能知道必得这地为业呢？
chapters.en: And he said, Lord GOD, whereby shall I know that I shall inherit it?

chapters.zh: 他说：你为我取一只三年的母牛，一只三年的母山羊，一只三年的公绵羊，一只斑鸠，一只雏鸽。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Take me an heifer of three years old, and a she goat of three years old, and a ram of three years old, and a turtledove, and a young pigeon.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰就取了这些来，每样劈开，分成两半，一半对著一半地摆列，只有鸟没有劈开。
chapters.en: And he took unto him all these, and divided them in the midst, and laid each piece one against another: but the birds divided he not.

chapters.zh: 有鸷鸟下来，落在那死畜的肉上，亚伯兰就把他吓飞了。
chapters.en: And when the fowls came down upon the carcases, Abram drove them away.

chapters.zh: 日头正落的时候，亚伯兰沉沉地睡了；忽然有惊人的大黑暗落在他身上。
chapters.en: And when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and, lo, an horror of great darkness fell upon him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对亚伯兰说：你要的确知道，你的后裔必寄居别人的地，又服事那地的人；那地的人要苦待他们四百年。
chapters.en: And he said unto Abram, Know of a surety that thy seed shall be a stranger in a land not theirs, and shall serve them; and they shall afflict them four hundred years;

chapters.zh: 并且他们所要服事的那国，我要惩罚，后来他们必带著许多财物从那里出来。
chapters.en: And also that nation, whom they shall serve, will I judge: and afterward shall they come out with great substance.

chapters.zh: 但你要享大寿数，平平安安地归到你列祖那里，被人埋葬。
chapters.en: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace; thou shalt be buried in a good old age.

chapters.zh: 到了第四代，他们必回到此地，因为亚摩利人的罪孽还没有满盈。
chapters.en: But in the fourth generation they shall come hither again: for the iniquity of the Amorites not yet full.

chapters.zh: 日落天黑，不料有冒烟的炉并烧著的火把从那些肉块中经过。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that, when the sun went down, and it was dark, behold a smoking furnace, and a burning lamp that passed between those pieces.

chapters.zh: 当那日，耶和华与亚伯兰立约，说：我已赐给你的后裔，从埃及河直到伯拉大河之地，
chapters.en: In the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates:

chapters.zh: 就是基尼人、基尼洗人、甲摩尼人、
chapters.en: The Kenites, and the Kenizzites, and the Kadmonites,

chapters.zh: 赫人、比利洗人、利乏音人、
chapters.en: And the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaims,

chapters.zh: 亚摩利人、迦南人、革迦撒人、耶布斯人之地。
chapters.en: And the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰的妻子撒莱不给他生儿女。撒莱有一个使女，名叫夏甲，是埃及人。
chapters.en: Now Sarai Abram's wife bare him no children: and she had an handmaid, an Egyptian, whose name Hagar.

chapters.zh: 撒莱对亚伯兰说：耶和华使我不能生育。求你和我的使女同房，或者我可以因他得孩子 ( 原文作被建立 )。亚伯兰听从了撒莱的话。
chapters.en: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.

chapters.zh: 于是亚伯兰的妻子撒莱将使女埃及人夏甲给了丈夫为妾；那时亚伯兰在迦南已经住了十年。
chapters.en: And Sarai Abram's wife took Hagar her maid the Egyptian, after Abram had dwelt ten years in the land of Canaan, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰与夏甲同房，夏甲就怀了孕；他见自己有孕，就小看他的主母。
chapters.en: And he went in unto Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.

chapters.zh: 撒莱对亚伯兰说：我因你受屈。我将我的使女放在你怀中，他见自己有了孕，就小看我。愿耶和华在你我中间判断。
chapters.en: And Sarai said unto Abram, My wrong upon thee: I have given my maid into thy bosom; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes: the LORD judge between me and thee.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰对撒莱说：使女在你手下，你可以随意待他。撒莱苦待他，他就从撒莱面前逃走了。
chapters.en: But Abram said unto Sarai, Behold, thy maid in thy hand; do to her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai dealt hardly with her, she fled from her face.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者在旷野书珥路上的水泉旁遇见他，
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.

chapters.zh: 对他说：撒莱的使女夏甲，你从那里来？要往那里去？夏甲说：我从我的主母撒莱面前逃出来。
chapters.en: And he said, Hagar, Sarai's maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou go? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistress Sarai.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者对他说：你回到你主母那里，服在他手下；
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.

chapters.zh: 又说：我必使你的后裔极其繁多，甚至不可胜数；
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.

chapters.zh: 并说：你如今怀孕要生一个儿子，可以给他起名叫以实玛利，因为耶和华听见了你的苦情。( 以实玛利就是神听见的意思 )
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Behold, thou with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the LORD hath heard thy affliction.

chapters.zh: 他为人必像野驴。他的手要攻打人，人的手也要攻打他；他必住在众弟兄的东边。
chapters.en: And he will be a wild man; his hand against every man, and every man's hand against him; and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.

chapters.zh: 夏甲就称那对他说话的耶和华为看顾人的神。因而说：在这里我也看见那看顾我的么？
chapters.en: And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?

chapters.zh: 所以这井名叫庇耳拉海莱。这井正在加低斯和巴列中间。
chapters.en: Wherefore the well was called Beerlahairoi; behold, between Kadesh and Bered.

chapters.zh: 后来夏甲给亚伯兰生了一个儿子；亚伯兰给他起名叫以实玛利。
chapters.en: And Hagar bare Abram a son: and Abram called his son's name, which Hagar bare, Ishmael.

chapters.zh: 夏甲给亚伯兰生以实玛利的时候，亚伯兰年八十六岁。
chapters.en: And Abram fourscore and six years old, when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰年九十九岁的时候，耶和华向他显现，对他说：我是全能的神。你当在我面前作完全人，
chapters.en: And when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the LORD appeared to Abram, and said unto him, I the Almighty God; walk before me, and be thou perfect.

chapters.zh: 我就与你立约，使你的后裔极其繁多。
chapters.en: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.

chapters.zh: 亚伯兰俯伏在地；神又对他说：
chapters.en: And Abram fell on his face: and God talked with him, saying,

chapters.zh: 我与你立约：你要作多国的父。
chapters.en: As for me, behold, my covenant with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.

chapters.zh: 从此以后，你的名不再叫亚伯兰，要叫亚伯拉罕，因为我已立你作多国的父。
chapters.en: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham; for a father of many nations have I made thee.

chapters.zh: 我必使你的后裔极其繁多；国度从你而立，君王从你而出。
chapters.en: And I will make thee exceeding fruitful, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.

chapters.zh: 我要与你并你世世代代的后裔坚立我的约，作永远的约，是要作你和你后裔的神。
chapters.en: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.

chapters.zh: 我要将你现在寄居的地，就是迦南全地，赐给你和你的后裔永远为业，我也必作他们的神。
chapters.en: And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.

chapters.zh: 神又对亚伯拉罕说：你和你的后裔必世世代代遵守我的约。
chapters.en: And God said unto Abraham, Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore, thou, and thy seed after thee in their generations.

chapters.zh: 你们所有的男子都要受割礼；这就是我与你并你的后裔所立的约，是你们所当遵守的。
chapters.en: This my covenant, which ye shall keep, between me and you and thy seed after thee; Every man child among you shall be circumcised.

chapters.zh: 你们都要受割礼 ( 原文作割阳皮；14，23，24，25节同 )；这是我与你们立约的证据。
chapters.en: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant betwixt me and you.

chapters.zh: 你们世世代代的男子，无论是家里生的，是在你后裔之外用银子从外人买的，生下来第八日，都要受割礼。
chapters.en: And he that is eight days old shall be circumcised among you, every man child in your generations, he that is born in the house, or bought with money of any stranger, which not of thy seed.

chapters.zh: 你家里生的和你用银子买的，都必须受割礼。这样，我的约就立在你们肉体上作永远的约。
chapters.en: He that is born in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant.

chapters.zh: 但不受割礼的男子必从民中剪除，因他背了我的约。
chapters.en: And the uncircumcised man child whose flesh of his foreskin is not circumcised, that soul shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.

chapters.zh: 神又对亚伯拉罕说：你的妻子撒莱不可再叫撒莱，他的名要叫撒拉。
chapters.en: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah her name .

chapters.zh: 我必赐福给他，也要使你从他得一个儿子。我要赐福给他，他也要作多国之母；必有百姓的君王从他而出。
chapters.en: And I will bless her, and give thee a son also of her: yea, I will bless her, and she shall be of nations; kings of people shall be of her.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕就俯伏在地喜笑，心里说：一百岁的人还能得孩子么？撒拉已经九十岁了，还能生养么？
chapters.en: Then Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, Shall be born unto him that is an hundred years old? and shall Sarah, that is ninety years old, bear?

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕对神说：但愿以实玛利活在你面前。
chapters.en: And Abraham said unto God, O that Ishmael might live before thee!

chapters.zh: 神说：不然，你妻子撒拉要给你生一个儿子，你要给他起名叫以撒。我要与他坚定所立的约，作他后裔永远的约。
chapters.en: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall bear thee a son indeed; and thou shalt call his name Isaac: and I will establish my covenant with him for an everlasting covenant, with his seed after him.

chapters.zh: 至于以实玛利，我也应允你：我必赐福给他，使他昌盛，极其繁多。他必生十二个族长；我也要使他成为大国。
chapters.en: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee: Behold, I have blessed him, and will make him fruitful, and will multiply him exceedingly; twelve princes shall he beget, and I will make him a great nation.

chapters.zh: 到明年这时节，撒拉必给你生以撒，我要与他坚定所立的约。
chapters.en: But my covenant will I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall bear unto thee at this set time in the next year.

chapters.zh: 神和亚伯拉罕说完了话，就离开他上升去了。
chapters.en: And he left off talking with him, and God went up from Abraham.

chapters.zh: 正当那日，亚伯拉罕遵著神的命，给他的儿子以实玛利和家里的一切男子，无论是在家里生的，是用银子买的，都行了割礼。
chapters.en: And Abraham took Ishmael his son, and all that were born in his house, and all that were bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham's house; and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin in the selfsame day, as God had said unto him.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕受割礼的时候年九十九岁。
chapters.en: And Abraham ninety years old and nine, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

chapters.zh: 他儿子以实玛利受割礼的时候年十三岁。
chapters.en: And Ishmael his son thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

chapters.zh: 正当那日，亚伯拉罕和他儿子以实玛利一同受了割礼。
chapters.en: In the selfsame day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his son.

chapters.zh: 家里所有的人，无论是在家里生的，是用银子从外人买的，也都一同受了割礼。
chapters.en: And all the men of his house, born in the house, and bought with money of the stranger, were circumcised with him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在幔利橡树那里向亚伯拉罕显现出来。那时正热，亚伯拉罕坐在帐棚门口，
chapters.en: And the LORD appeared unto him in the plains of Mamre: and he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day;

chapters.zh: 举目观看，见有三个人在对面站著。他一见，就从帐棚门口跑去迎接他们，俯伏在地，
chapters.en: And he lift up his eyes and looked, and, lo, three men stood by him: and when he saw , he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself toward the ground,

chapters.zh: 说：我主，我若在你眼前蒙恩，求你不要离开仆人往前去。
chapters.en: And said, My Lord, if now I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away, I pray thee, from thy servant:

chapters.zh: 容我拿点水来，你们洗洗脚，在树下歇息歇息。
chapters.en: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree:

chapters.zh: 我再拿一点饼来，你们可以加添心力，然后往前去。你们既到仆人这里来，理当如此。他们说：就照你说的行罢。
chapters.en: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on: for therefore are ye come to your servant. And they said, So do, as thou hast said.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕急忙进帐棚见撒拉，说：你速速拿三细亚细面调和作饼。
chapters.en: And Abraham hastened into the tent unto Sarah, and said, Make ready quickly three measures of fine meal, knead , and make cakes upon the hearth.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕又跑到牛群里，牵了一只又嫩又好的牛犊来，交给仆人，仆人急忙预备好了。
chapters.en: And Abraham ran unto the herd, and fetcht a calf tender and good, and gave unto a young man; and he hasted to dress it.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕又取了奶油和奶，并预备好的牛犊来，摆在他们面前，自己在树下站在旁边，他们就吃了。
chapters.en: And he took butter, and milk, and the calf which he had dressed, and set before them; and he stood by them under the tree, and they did eat.

chapters.zh: 他们问亚伯拉罕说：你妻子撒拉在那里？他说：在帐棚里。
chapters.en: And they said unto him, Where Sarah thy wife? And he said, Behold, in the tent.

chapters.zh: 三人中有一位说：到明年这时候，我必要回到你这里；你的妻子撒拉必生一个儿子。撒拉在那人后边的帐棚门口也听见了这话。
chapters.en: And he said, I will certainly return unto thee according to the time of life; and, lo, Sarah thy wife shall have a son. And Sarah heard in the tent door, which behind him.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕和撒拉年纪老迈，撒拉的月经已断绝了。
chapters.en: Now Abraham and Sarah old well stricken in age; it ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.

chapters.zh: 撒拉心里暗笑，说：我既已衰败，我主也老迈，岂能有这喜事呢？
chapters.en: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, After I am waxed old shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?

chapters.zh: 耶和华对亚伯拉罕说：撒拉为甚么暗笑，说：我既已年老，果真能生养么？
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Abraham, Wherefore did Sarah laugh, saying, Shall I of a surety bear a child, which am old?

chapters.zh: 耶和华岂有难成的事么？到了日期，明年这时候，我必回到你这里，撒拉必生一个儿子。
chapters.en: Is any thing too hard for the LORD? At the time appointed I will return unto thee, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.

chapters.zh: 撒拉就害怕，不承认，说：我没有笑。那位说：不然，你实在笑了。
chapters.en: Then Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not; for she was afraid. And he said, Nay; but thou didst laugh.

chapters.zh: 三人就从那里起行，向所多玛观看，亚伯拉罕也与他们同行，要送他们一程。
chapters.en: And the men rose up from thence, and looked toward Sodom: and Abraham went with them to bring them on the way.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我所要作的事岂可瞒著亚伯拉罕呢？
chapters.en: And the LORD said, Shall I hide from Abraham that thing which I do;

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕必要成为强大的国；地上的万国都必因他得福。
chapters.en: Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?

chapters.zh: 我眷顾他，为要叫他吩咐他的众子和他的眷属遵守我的道，秉公行义，使我所应许亚伯拉罕的话都成就了。
chapters.en: For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment; that the LORD may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：所多玛和蛾摩拉的罪恶甚重，声闻于我。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous;

chapters.zh: 我现在要下去，察看他们所行的，果然尽像那达到我耳中的声音一样么？若是不然，我也必知道。
chapters.en: I will go down now, and see whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come unto me; and if not, I will know.

chapters.zh: 二人转身离开那里，向所多玛去；但亚伯拉罕仍旧站在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And the men turned their faces from thence, and went toward Sodom: but Abraham stood yet before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕近前来，说：无论善恶，你都要剿灭么？
chapters.en: And Abraham drew near, and said, Wilt thou also destroy the righteous with the wicked?

chapters.zh: 假若那城里有五十个义人，你还剿灭那地方么？不为城里这五十个义人饶恕其中的人么？
chapters.en: Peradventure there be fifty righteous within the city: wilt thou also destroy and not spare the place for the fifty righteous that therein?

chapters.zh: 将义人与恶人同杀，将义人与恶人一样看待，这断不是你所行的。审判全地的主岂不行公义么？
chapters.en: That be far from thee to do after this manner, to slay the righteous with the wicked: and that the righteous should be as the wicked, that be far from thee: Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我若在所多玛城里见有五十个义人，我就为他们的缘故饶恕那地方的众人。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, If I find in Sodom fifty righteous within the city, then I will spare all the place for their sakes.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕说：我虽然是灰尘，还敢对主说话。
chapters.en: And Abraham answered and said, Behold now, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, which dust and ashes:

chapters.zh: 假若这五十个义人短了五个，你就因为短了五个毁灭全城么？他说：我在那里若见有四十五个，也不毁灭那城。
chapters.en: Peradventure there shall lack five of the fifty righteous: wilt thou destroy all the city for five? And he said, If I find there forty and five, I will not destroy .

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕又对他说：假若在那里见有四十个怎么样呢？他说：为这四十个的缘故，我也不作这事。
